title: oroya peruviana var. neoperuviana
intro: Oroya peruviana var. neoperuviana, also known as Neoperuvian cinchona, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America. It is a variety of the cinchona tree, which has been historically used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat and prevent malaria due to its high concentration of quinine alkaloids. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and its health benefits also include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark of the Neoperuvian cinchona is typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
